description_long: This is a medium-sized, roughly rectangular slab of gray stone with a layered, stratified appearance. The rock features a flat upper surface with subtle, natural cracks and a slightly raised section along one edge, suggesting sedimentary origins. Its sides are uneven and jagged, with visible horizontal striations and a few angular breaks, giving it a rugged, natural look. The coloration is predominantly a cool, light gray with some faint white veining and weathering marks. The bottom appears more irregular and less finished, while the top is broad and could serve as a resting or sitting surface. This type of rock is commonly found in landscaped urban parks or as a decorative barrier along sidewalks or plazas.
description: A flat, layered gray stone slab with a rugged, stratified texture and a broad, slightly uneven top surface. Its natural, weathered appearance makes it suitable for use as a barrier or decorative element in urban settings.
description_view_0: The rock appears as a flat, rectangular slab with a prominent layered edge and a slightly raised section on the right.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rock's thickness and the pronounced horizontal striations on its side are visible, with a more blocky, solid appearance.
description_view_2: This view highlights the angular break on the right side and the overall rectangular shape, with the flat top surface clearly visible.
description_view_3: Looking from above, the rock's flatness and the subtle cracks on its surface are most apparent, with the raised edge on the upper right.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk area

license_info.creator_display_name: UQ School of Earth and Environmental Sciences
